item.title: China’s AI Stocks Gain as State Media Tout Rising Token Usage
item.description: Chinese artificial intelligence service stocks rallied after state media highlighted a sharp increase in domestic AI model adoption and a surge in token usage they generate.

item.title: India Has Bought 60 Million Barrels of Russian Oil for April
item.description: Indian refiners have bought about 60 million barrels of Russian oil for delivery next month, according to people familiar with the matter, easing supply concerns as the Middle East war chokes flows.

item.title: Scion of $6 Billion Carlyle Fortune Defies Private Equity Slump
item.description: David Rubenstein made one of the world’s biggest buyout fortunes with bets on everything from oil pipelines to pet food. Today, the Carlyle Group Inc. co-founder’s middle child is working to build her own reputation in the industry with a different approach.

item.title: Fire at Russia’s Ust-Luga Oil Port on Baltic After Drone Attack
item.description: A fire was being contained at Russia’s Ust-Luga port on Wednesday after 56 drones were destroyed over the Leningrad region, local governor Alexander Drozdenko said, the latest strike on the country’s oil export infrastructure.

item.title: AI Demand Is Shielding China’s Booming Trade From Iran War Shock
item.description: An investment boom in artificial intelligence has kept China’s trade volumes on a path to exceed last year’s record levels, offsetting disruptions from higher oil prices in the weeks after war broke out in Iran.

item.title: Iran War Could Be Making of the Petroyuan, Deutsche Bank Says
item.description: The war in Iran is testing the dollar’s role as the currency for global oil trade, with one long-term consequence being a potential shift to using more Chinese yuan, according to Deutsche Bank.

item.title: Trade Any Oil Shock on Stocks at Open Then Steer Clear, UBS Says
item.description: Investors seeking to buy or sell Asian stocks following any spike in oil prices should aim to execute orders in the first few minutes of the trading day and then steer clear, according to UBS AG.

item.title: Chinese Investors’ Day Trading Signals Weak Hong Kong Conviction
item.description: Chinese mainland investors’ fund flows into Hong Kong stocks have sharply fluctuated in recent weeks, underscoring a lack of conviction to build long-term positions.

item.title: Chinese Bonds Are Appealing as Reserve Assets, Gavekal Says
item.description: Chinese government bonds are emerging as a viable alternative reserve asset after holding up through recent geopolitical shocks including the Iran war, according to Gavekal Research.

item.title: Iran War Risks Prying Asia’s Tech, Consumer Shares Further Apart
item.description: The Iran conflict may help Asian technology shares further outperform their consumer peers, as concerns rise that the war’s inflationary impact will outweigh its damage on artificial intelligence supply chains.

item.title: Copper Rises as US Push to End War Buoys Industrial Metals
item.description: Copper and most other metals rose as optimism around Washington’s diplomatic push to end the war in the Middle East boosted risk appetite.

item.title: Fortescue Chairman Urges China to End Iron Ore Buying Strategy
item.description: Fortescue Ltd.’s Executive Chairman Andrew Forrest said China’s state-backed iron buyer should stop using tactics aimed at negotiating lower prices for the key steel ingredient.

item.title: Indonesia Rupiah Rises Most in Six Months, Stocks Gain in Reopen
item.description: The Indonesian rupiah and stocks gained as domestic markets reopened after a week-long holiday, buoyed by hopes over the Trump administration’s push to ease Iran war tensions.

item.title: Indian Shares Show Signs of Bottoming Out Even as Iran War Rages
item.description: Derivatives data suggests selling pressure is easing.

item.title: RBA Shifting Focus on Digital Tokens From If to How, Jones Says
item.description: Australia’s central bank has shifted its focus from whether wholesale digital token will play a role in the financial system to how this can be implemented, as it looks to unlock efficiency gains following a pilot project, according to Assistant Governor Brad Jones.

item.title: Meta Offers Top Execs Stock Options for First Time Since IPO
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is offering top executives stock options for the first time since its 2012 IPO — an effort to retain and compensate executives as the company continues to spend aggressively to compete in the heated AI race.

item.title: South Korea Shifts to Crisis Mode as Iran Oil Shock Intensifies
item.description: South Korea is stepping up contingency planning for a worst-case Middle East scenario, with Prime Minister Kim Min-seok warning that the government must strengthen its preemptive response systems as the conflict shows signs of persisting.

item.title: Sri Lanka Keeps Policy Rate Unchanged as Oil Cost Risks Loom
item.description: Sri Lanka’s central bank held its benchmark rate unchanged for a fifth straight meeting, with rising oil prices from the Middle East conflict adding to inflationary pressure.

item.title: Vingroup’s Hospitality Arm Seeks $300 Million in Private Debt
item.description: The hospitality unit of Vingroup JSC is seeking a private credit loan of as much as $300 million for refinancing, people familiar with the matter said, the latest in a series of borrowings raised by the Vietnamese conglomerate.

item.title: Disney CEO’s First Week Marred by ‘Fortnite,’ OpenAI Woes
item.description: Walt Disney Co. Chief Executive Officer Josh D’Amaro hasn’t even been been in his new job for one week and he’s already seen two, billion-dollar technology bets falter —- with one of them unraveling entirely.

item.title: Japan-Based SmartNews Is Said to Eye October Listing in Tokyo
item.description: SmartNews Inc., a Japanese news aggregation app operator, is considering a Tokyo listing as soon as October for its initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Heineken to Move Singapore Beer Production to Malaysia, Vietnam
item.description: Heineken NV will pare back large-scale beer production in Singapore by 2027 as it shifts output to breweries in Malaysia and Vietnam as part of a broader regional reorganization.

item.title: Taiwan Stocks Join Regional Rebound on Iran De-Escalation Hopes
item.description: Taiwan’s equity benchmark Taiex rises as much as 3.3% amid a broad Asian market rebound on expectations of a de-escalation in the Iran conflict.

item.title: New Zealand Has No Appetite for Unaffordable Responses to Shock
item.description: New Zealand will adopt a careful approach to the fuel crisis because it doesn’t have the fiscal headroom for unrestrained spending to ease the pain of higher pump prices, according to Finance Minister Nicola Willis.

item.title: Merck Nears Deal to Buy Terns Pharmaceuticals
item.description: Merck & Co. is in advanced talks to buy drugmaker Terns Pharmaceuticals Inc., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Thai AI Startup Amity Raises $100 Million in Push Toward IPO
item.description: Thailand’s Amity, which builds and integrates generative AI technology for businesses, raised $100 million as it gears up for a stock-market debut and accelerates its product rollout.

item.title: SpaceX to File for US IPO Soon as This Week, Information Says
item.description: SpaceX aims to file a prospectus for an initial public offering as soon as this week, the Information reported, kicking off one of the year’s most-anticipated market debuts.

item.title: Indonesia Markets Reopen as Shifting Iran Headlines Spur Caution
item.description: Indonesia’s markets are likely to grapple with fluctuating sentiment when they reopen Wednesday after a week-long holiday, as investors weigh rapidly shifting headlines on the Iran war and persistent concerns over fiscal and governance risks.

item.title: RBNZ’s Conway Sees Economic Slack Shaping Response to Oil Shock
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank sees lingering slack in the economy that will shape how aggressively it responds to the inflationary aftershocks of higher oil prices, Chief Economist Paul Conway said.

item.title: Australian Inflation Was Elevated Even Before Iran Oil Shock
item.description: Australian inflation remained elevated in February, even before the Iran war disrupted Middle East energy supplies and sent the cost of gasoline soaring, highlighting persistent price pressures in the economy.
